events.title: US and Iran Reach Peace Deal
events.desc: The United States and Iran have reportedly reached a comprehensive deal to end their war, with a signing ceremony planned for June 19 in Switzerland. This agreement includes an immediate and permanent termination of military operations on all fronts, including in Lebanon. The deal comes after extensive negotiations and signifies a potential de-escalation in the region.

events.title: Israel Strikes Hezbollah Targets in Beirut
events.desc: Israel's military conducted strikes against Hezbollah targets in Beirut's southern suburbs following Hezbollah's firing of projectiles into northern Israel. The Lebanese state news agency reported two fatalities. This incident marks a significant escalation amidst ongoing efforts to secure a US-Iran peace deal.

events.title: Three Dead in Israeli Strikes on Beirut Suburbs
events.desc: Three people were killed in Israeli strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs, carried out in response to Hezbollah's alleged fire at northern Israel. The Israeli military also conducted broader strikes across southern Lebanon. This escalation occurred as expectations of a US-Iran peace deal rose, though Iran's chief negotiator expressed doubts about continuing talks.

events.title: Iran Team Arrives in US for World Cup
events.desc: Iran's national soccer team arrived in Los Angeles for their World Cup opener against New Zealand. This marks the team's first arrival in the United States. The team's presence is shadowed by political tensions and potential protests from Iranian-Americans.

events.title: Iran Reports Draft US Deal Includes Nuclear Limits and Oil Sanctions Waiver
events.desc: A senior Iranian official stated that a draft memorandum of understanding with the U.S. includes provisions for Iran's nuclear work, the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and U.S. waivers on oil sanctions. The final deal is subject to discussion within 60 days of agreement.

events.title: Lebanon Reports Strikes as US-Iran Deal Nears
events.desc: Lebanon reported Israeli strikes in its southern regions and the east on Saturday, with the Israeli army issuing evacuation warnings for Nabatieh and other locations. These strikes occurred as the US and Iran indicated proximity to a deal to end the Middle East war, which could involve Lebanon.

events.title: Israel Strikes Hezbollah in Beirut Suburbs Amidst Truce
events.desc: Israel carried out strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs, resulting in casualties, days after a US-brokered truce. The Israeli military stated the attacks were in response to Hezbollah firing at Israeli territory. This action raises concerns about the durability of the ceasefire and the ongoing peace process.

events.title: 17 Killed in Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Reports indicate that Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon have resulted in the deaths of 17 individuals, with nine fatalities reported in Tayr Debba. This incident follows recent escalations and underscores the volatile security situation in the region.

events.title: Three Indian Sailors Killed in US Strike on Oil Tanker
events.desc: Three Indian sailors died when a U.S. military strike hit an oil tanker, identified as Palau-flagged. The U.S. military stated the action was taken after the tanker failed to comply with directions, highlighting risks to maritime operations in contested waters.

events.title: Iran and Israel Pause Strikes, Warn of Retaliation
events.desc: Both Israel and Iran have announced a pause in their strikes, with Israel's Prime Minister stating they are holding fire 'at the moment' and Iran's armed forces confirming a cessation of military action. However, both sides have issued warnings of retaliation if the ceasefire is breached again.

events.title: One Dead, Five Injured in Shooting Incident in Israel
events.desc: A shooting incident in Israel resulted in one fatality and five injuries. The suspect reportedly opened fire from a vehicle at multiple locations before being killed by authorities. This incident adds to the security concerns in the region.

events.title: Funeral Held for Baby Killed by Israeli Troops
events.desc: A funeral was held for a seven-month-old baby who was fatally shot by Israeli troops in the occupied West Bank. The incident has drawn significant international attention and condemnation, highlighting ongoing tensions and violence in the region.

events.title: Grain Smuggling from Ukraine to Libya Tracked
events.desc: A new technique has been employed to track grain smuggling operations, including the vessel Grumant, which was observed at the occupied Ukrainian Port of Feodosia and later docked in Benghazi, Libya. This highlights ongoing illicit trade routes despite international scrutiny.

events.title: Banned Russian Cluster Munitions Found in Mali
events.desc: Unexploded Russian-made cluster munition bomblets have been discovered in a village in northern Mali, coinciding with the Malian military's announcement of airstrikes. This finding is significant as Mali is a state party to the Convention on Cluster Munitions.

events.title: Demolitions Continue in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Satellite imagery reveals ongoing demolitions across southern Lebanon, with at least 46 out of 54 towns and villages within the Israeli Defense Forces' 'Yellow Line' showing heavy damage or complete flattening. This destruction occurs despite a fragile ceasefire.
